issue.title: Desktop: Resolves #14763: Add search support for settings screen
issue.body:
Resolves #14763

## Problem

The desktop configuration screen currently does not provide a way to search settings, which makes it difficult to quickly locate specific options when many settings are available. Users must manually scan through sections even when they already know the setting name or part of its description.

## Solution

This change adds a search field to the desktop settings screen that filters visible settings by matching setting labels, descriptions, and section titles in a case-insensitive way.

The existing SearchInput component is reused so behaviour remains consistent with other parts of the application. The search field is placed in the sidebar at the top, like in the mobile app, and includes a clear button similar to the regular search field. The right panel displays all matching settings, while sections without matching settings remain visible in the sidebar but are grayed out and not clickable. By default, all matching settings are displayed, and users can click sections to filter to just those sections.

Filtering logic was extracted into a helper to keep ConfigScreen simpler and testable. Empty and null label/description values are handled safely to avoid crashes. Search also applies to plugin settings, and the search term is highlighted in labels, descriptions, and section titles.

This change only affects filtering behaviour in the desktop configuration screen and does not modify setting values or persistence.

## Test Plan

* Added unit tests for settings search matching logic  
* Open Tools → Options  
* Verify all settings appear when search is empty  
* Verify the search field appears at the top of the sidebar  
* Search using a setting label (for example: language)  
* Search using text present in a setting description  
* Search using a section title  
* Verify matching settings appear in the right panel  
* Verify sections without matching settings remain visible but grayed out and not clickable  
* Verify clicking a section filters results to that section  
* Verify plugin settings are included in search results  
* Verify matching text is highlighted in labels, descriptions, and section titles  
* Clear the search field and verify full settings list returns

## Tested with NVDA on Windows.

*The new settings search field is reachable via keyboard, announced correctly as an editable search field, and can be used to filter settings without a mouse.

*Matching results remain navigable by keyboard, and focus behaves normally when moving between the sidebar and filtered settings.


## Video







https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9de697d-f1f1-4d9a-8e01-fee61602be5f





## AI Assistance Disclosure

AI-assisted: Yes

Used AI assistance for:

* reviewing PR description wording
* refining search helper edge-case handling

All code, testing, and final verification were manually reviewed before submission.

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds a search feature to the desktop Config screen: new search state and handlers, search/filter/highlight utilities, sidebar search input with enable/disable section logic and keyboard navigation, search-aware rendering for settings/plugins, and a “No settings match your search.” state. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Main Config screen**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/ConfigScreen.tsx`|Adds `searchQuery`/`searchSelectedSectionName`, search handlers, section-filtering and matching logic, search-aware rendering flow, adjusted sidebar selection during search, conditional custom-screen rendering, and “no results” handling.| |**Search utils & tests**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/configScreenUtils.ts`, `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/configScreenUtilsImpl.tsx`, `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/ConfigScreen.search.test.ts`|New re-export entry-point and implementation for normalization/matching/highlighting (`settingMatchesSearch`, `highlightSearchText`, `searchQueryIsEmpty`) plus Jest tests covering matching scenarios.| |**Sidebar & tab UI**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/Sidebar.tsx`, `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/styles/setting-tab-panel.scss`|Sidebar accepts search props, renders `SearchInput`, computes enabled/disabled tabs with `isSectionMatch`, restricts keyboard navigation to matched tabs, and adds `.-search-results` modifier for tab-panel layout.| |**Setting controls & plugins**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/controls/*`, `.../controls/plugins/*`|Prop additions (`searchQuery` / `highlightQuery`) across `SettingComponent`, `SettingLabel`, `SettingDescription`, `SettingHeader`, `PluginBox`, `PluginsStates`; components use `highlightSearchText`; `PluginsStates` exposes `getSearchText()`.| |**Search input styling**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/lib/SearchInput/SearchInput.tsx`|Adjusts search input/icon colours and placeholder styling; no behavioural changes.| |**Styles**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/style.scss`, `packages/app-desktop/gui/ConfigScreen/styles/setting-tab-panel.scss`|Adds `.config-screen mark.config-search-highlight` styling and `.setting-tab-panel.-search-results` modifier to adjust layout for search results.| |**Build/test ignores**
`.eslintignore`, `.gitignore`|Ignore generated JS artifacts for new search utils/tests from linting and TypeScript build outputs.| |**Mobile plugin assets**
`packages/app-mobile/pluginAssets/index.js`, `packages/app-mobile/pluginAssets/index.web.js`|Updated asset manifest `hash` and minor reordering/removal of `abc/abc_render.js` entries.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User participant Config as ConfigScreen participant Utils as SearchUtils participant Sidebar as Sidebar participant View as SettingsView User->>Config: Type query / Click clear Config->>Utils: Normalise query, split terms, compute matches Utils-->>Config: filteredSections, matchingSectionNames Config->>Sidebar: update searchQuery, searchStarted, provide isSectionMatch callback Config->>View: compute sectionsToRender, pass searchQuery for highlights alt no results View-->>User: "No settings match your search." else results View-->>User: Render matching settings with highlighted matches end ``` ## Suggested labels `accessibility` ## Suggested reviewers - personalizedrefrigerator

The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |

Mobile asset updates appear to be auto-generated build artifacts. No extraneous modifications detected. | | Pr Description Must Follow Guidelines | ✅ Passed | The pull request description includes clear sections addressing Problem (issue `#14763` requesting search functionality in settings), Solution (detailed implementation of search with filtering and highlighting), and Test Plan (manual accessibility testing with NVDA on Windows, video, and AI disclosure). The comments history explicitly confirms the contributor updated the PR description to include Problem/Solution/Test Plan sections as per the GSoC PR guidelines, and the provided PR summary demonstrates all three required elements are present and properly documented. |

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/issues/14763

If you would like to continue working on this pull request, please check the spec.

> 
> #14763
> 
> If you would like to continue working on this pull request, please check the spec.

Done👍

Thank you for working on this!

Since this modifies an important part of the desktop app's UI:
- How accessible is the new implementation?
- Would you be willing to test the change with a screen reader? (See [Joplin: Testing with a screen reader](https://joplinapp.org/help/dev/accessibility#testing-with-a-screen-reader)).
  - If not, I can try to do so at some point in the near future, but it may take me a few days.

> Thank you for working on this!
> 
> Since this modifies an important part of the desktop app's UI:
> 
> * How accessible is the new implementation?
> * Would you be willing to test the change with a screen reader? (See [Joplin: Testing with a screen reader](https://joplinapp.org/help/dev/accessibility#testing-with-a-screen-reader)).
>   
>   * If not, I can try to do so at some point in the near future, but it may take me a few days.

Tested with NVDA on Windows and added a short video as well.

The search field is reachable by keyboard, announced correctly by the screen reader, and filtering works without using a mouse. Matching results remain keyboard accessible, and focus behaves normally while navigating between the sidebar and filtered settings.

> Tested with NVDA on Windows and added a short video as well.

Thank you!! Possible issue: The keyboard seems to skip entries in the tab control. UI components that can be activated with a mouse should also be usable from a keyboard (see [the WCAG 2.2 accessibility guidelines](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/keyboard)).

For reference, the settings tabs should follow the [ARIA tab pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tabs/) (at least, when not searching).

I've also [enabled automated accessibility checks](https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/pull/14793) for the settings screen, which should help catch certain accessibility issues.

> I've also https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/pull/14793 for the settings screen, which should help catch certain accessibility issues.

I've merged recent changes from `dev`: The new accessibility tests should now run in CI.

> I've merged recent changes from dev: The new accessibility tests should now run in CI. The accessibility checker has flagged two potential issues: - Elements with [`role="tablist"`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/tablist_role) cannot contain `button`s or `input`s. - Based on the [spec](https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tablist:~:text=composite-,Allowed,tab), `tablist` should only contain elements with the `tab` role. - The settings search input has insufficient text contrast. - Note: The [WebAIM contrast checker](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/) might be helpful here.

Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1", + "relatedNodes": Array [ + Object { + "html": "", + "target": Array [ + "input[placeholder=\"Search settings...\"]", + ], + }, + Object { + "html": "
", + "target": Array [ + ".sc-JrDLc", + ], + }, + ], + }, + ], + "failureSummary": "Fix any of the following: + Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.29 (foreground color: #32373f, background color: #989ba0, font size: 9.0pt (12px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1", + "html": "", + "impact": "serious", + "none": Array [], + "target": Array [ + "input[placeholder=\"Search settings...\"]", + ], + }, + ], + "tags": Array [ + "cat.color", + "wcag2aa", + "wcag143", + "TTv5", + "TT13.c", + "EN-301-549", + "EN-9.1.4.3", + "ACT", + "RGAAv4", + "RGAA-3.2.1", + ], + }, + ] ```

> > I've merged recent changes from dev: The new accessibility tests should now run in CI.
> 
> The accessibility checker has flagged two potential issues:
> 
> * Elements with [`role="tablist"`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/tablist_role) cannot contain `button`s or `input`s.
>   
>   * Based on the [spec](https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tablist:~:text=composite-,Allowed,tab), `tablist` should only contain elements with the `tab` role.
> * The settings search input has insufficient text contrast.
>   
>   * Note: The [WebAIM contrast checker](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/) might be helpful here.
> 
> Details

Thanks @personalizedrefrigerator  — I synced with the latest dev changes and addressed the two accessibility issues flagged by CI:

* moved the search input outside the tablist so the tablist now contains only tab elements
* adjusted the search input styling to improve text contrast

I also rechecked keyboard navigation for the sidebar/search flow locally. CI should reflect the updated state after this push.

> Thank you for working on this! I've tested the pull request locally. > > Observed issues: > > * The up/down arrows only sometimes changes the selected tab: > > Screencast.from.2026-03-23.08-52-36.webm > To reproduce: 1) start searching, 2) use the tab key to move keyboard focus to the tab list, and 3) press the up/down arrows. For me, this scrolls the tab list, rather than changes tabs. However, after clicking on the tab list, the arrow keys can successfully change the selected tab. > > * Low contrast in highlight marks while searching: It's difficult to read highlighted text in light mode (see [WCAG 2.2: Contrast/minimum](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html)): > Thanks for the detailed review sir, I’ve addressed the reported issues: * Fixed arrow key navigation in the sidebar tab list — up/down arrows now reliably change the selected tab when focused via keyboard and no longer scroll the list. * Improved contrast of search highlight marks to ensure readability (WCAG-compliant in both themes). * Replaced `React.createElement` usage with JSX for highlight rendering. I’ve re-tested keyboard navigation and search behavior locally, including keyboard-only interaction. I’ve also attached a short video demonstrating: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/393d680e-1779-45f5-aad7-9330a9fd04f2 * keyboard navigation with arrow keys * search + highlight visibility Please let me know if anything still needs adjustment.

Thanks for the suggestions @personalizedrefrigerator 

I've updated the `screenComp` assignment to a multi-line format for better readability.

I’m also refactoring the new inline styles to use RSCSS and aligning them with the guidelines in `spec/desktop_styling`.

Let me know if anything else stands out.

@personalizedrefrigerator Hi, I’ve addressed all the requested changes and updated the PR accordingly.

Would appreciate a re-review when you have time. Thanks!
Hello, unfortunately we no longer need this pull request as there's a duplicate here, that we will probably merge instead: https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/pull/14820
